TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 2 — moderate (~10 files touched, 3 modules: @yelli/db, @yelli/shared, apps/web). Single Opus session well within 30K budget per task. Direct execution chosen over Sonnet dispatch for the same reason as #2 and #3 — well-scoped, deterministic infra work where one-head-on-context for schema + tests + router + pages keeps the cross-file invariants (security.md AUTH DEFAULTS, no enumeration, single-use, security_version bump) coherent.

PHASE_7_TICKET_4_NOTES:
  - Token storage: separate PasswordResetToken table chosen over ephemeral User fields. Reasons: auditable (consumed_at marker preserved instead of deleting), supports multiple outstanding tokens, explicit single-use invariant, cascade-on-delete keeps cleanup automatic. Plaintext token (32B → 43-char base64url) never persists — only sha256(token) stored. Logged 🟤 decision.
  - Prisma migrate dev DATABASE_URL gap: Prisma CLI 5.22.0 does not auto-load `.env.dev`. Workaround: `env $(grep -v '^#' .env.dev | grep -E '^(DATABASE_URL|DB_)' | xargs) pnpm prisma migrate dev --name <slug>`. The db:seed script uses `tsx --env-file-if-exists` but Prisma CLI doesn't accept that flag. Logged 🔴 gotcha.
  - nodemailer peer-dep: 8.0.7 conflicts with next-auth ^6.6.5 and @auth/core ^7.0.7. Pinned to 6.9.x; one residual @auth/prisma-adapter warning accepted (email provider unused). If we ever wire Auth.js's email provider, revisit. Logged 🔴 gotcha.
  - vi.mock @/env extension: when a tRPC router reads `env.NEXT_PUBLIC_*`, the test must mock @/env in addition to data dependencies — env.ts validates at module-load time with NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL undefined → throw. Extends [[trpc-test-pattern]] from Phase 7 #2. Logged 🟤 decision.
  - Phase 7 #3 patterns reused cleanly: [[trpc-test-pattern]] ported to new procedures; [[shadcn-form-primitive]] from @yelli/ui used for _reset-form.tsx. Phase 7 #3's pattern locking is paying compound interest now — no schema-split debate, no UI-Rule-#4 retrofit, just import and go.
  - security_version invalidation working as designed: resetPassword transaction bumps the User.security_version Int field — Auth.js session callback (already in place from Phase 4 Part 5a) compares stale token versions and forces re-auth. End-to-end session invalidation on password change works without touching auth.config.
